## Course Overview
<br>

### Course Integrity
**This course is compiled automatically on _`r Sys.Date()`_ **
<br>
[![compilation-check](https://github.com/RockefellerUniversity/Intermediate_R/actions/workflows/compilation-check.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/RockefellerUniversity/Intermediate_R/actions/workflows/compilation-check.yml)



**The course is tested and available on MacOS, Windows and Ubuntu Linux for `r R.Version()$version.string`**
<br>
[![OS-check](https://github.com/RockefellerUniversity/Intermediate_R/actions/workflows/OS-check.yaml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/RockefellerUniversity/Intermediate_R/actions/workflows/OS-check.yaml)

<br>
<br>

### Overview

This course introduces a couple of methods to improve speed and efficiency while coding in R. We will introduce the Tidyverse and Data.table ecosystem of packages. Tidyverse packages allow for more readable code, more robust evaluation and a high-level of interoperability. While data.table is focused in raw speed allowing the handling of very large data. Finally we cover a variety of methods for parallelizing data analysis using BiocParallel and Futures. 

The course is presented as HTML presentations or single page document. Exercises and answer sheets are included after all subsections to practice techniques and provide future reference examples.

Course material and exercises are available to view as rendered HTML at https://rockefelleruniversity.github.io/Intermediate_R/.
All material is available to download under GPL v2 license.
